courseTitle: B2 TED TALKS第7堂 - New Year's Resolutions Dialogue

vocabulary.definition: careful thought about past events or decisions
vocabulary.example: Did you have some nice reflections over New Year's?

vocabulary.definition: a firm decision to do or stop doing something, often made at New Year
vocabulary.example: I made so many incredible New Year's resolutions.

vocabulary.definition: the position in which someone holds their body
vocabulary.example: My life changed when I fixed my posture last year.
I'm going to delete the dating apps because they make me miserable.

vocabulary.definition: a limit that defines acceptable behavior or personal space
vocabulary.example: This year, I'm putting up boundaries with my boss.

vocabulary.definition: to think deeply and carefully about something
vocabulary.example: Contemplate the relationship between attempting to change one's habits.

vocabulary.zh: 根深蒂固的；難以改變的
vocabulary.definition: firmly established and difficult to change or remove
vocabulary.example: Wrestling with a deep-rooted sense of some self-hatred.

vocabulary.zh: 頑皮的；不聽話的；（輕微）不乖的
vocabulary.definition: behaving badly; slightly improper or mischievous
vocabulary.example: Not like last year — that was naughty!

vocabulary.definition: a person who shares a house or apartment with others
vocabulary.example: We could do a housemate dinner with no phones.

vocabulary.definition: money that has been saved over time
vocabulary.example: I need to stop lying on the couch and eating into my savings.

vocabulary.definition: a small but noticeable touch of bright color
vocabulary.example: Life's too short — I'm gonna wear a pop of colour.

vocabulary.definition: to gradually use up money that has been saved
vocabulary.example: I need to stop lying on the couch and eating into my savings.

vocabulary.definition: to establish firm limits on what others can demand from you
vocabulary.example: She has so many demands but this year, I'm putting up boundaries.

vocabulary.definition: to continue doing something as planned without giving up
vocabulary.example: We're going to stick to these resolutions.

vocabulary.definition: something that fundamentally changes a situation or way of doing things
vocabulary.example: It's going to be a game changer.

sentences.sentence: Life's too short — I'm gonna wear a pop of colour.
sentences.zh: 人生苦短，我要穿一抹鮮豔的色彩。

sentences.sentence: My body needs to move so I should listen to that.
sentences.zh: 我的身體需要動，所以我應該聽從身體的聲音。

sentences.sentence: I need to stop lying on the couch and eating into my savings.
sentences.zh: 我得停止整天躺在沙發上、坐吃山空了。

sentences.sentence: It's going to be a game changer and we're going to stick to these resos.
sentences.zh: 這將會是個大改變，而且我們要堅守這些新年決心。

grammar.rule: Gonna / Gotta / Wanna — 口語縮讀
grammar.explanation: 口語中常把 going to → gonna、got to → gotta、want to → wanna，讓對話更自然。書寫中不建議使用，但聽力和口語中非常普遍，要能聽懂。
examples: I'm gonna wear a pop of colour. (= I'm going to wear...)
I gotta stop doing my baby voice. (= I've got to stop...)
We're gonna stick to these resos. (= We're going to stick...)

grammar.rule: Stop + V-ing vs. Stop + to V（停止做某事 vs. 停下來去做某事）
grammar.explanation: stop + 動名詞（V-ing）= 停止某個正在進行的行為；stop + to V = 停下來，轉而去做另一件事。兩個意思完全不同！
examples: I need to stop lying on the couch. (= 停止躺著這個習慣)
She stopped to think. (= 她停下來，開始思考)
I gotta stop doing my baby voice. (= 停止那個行為)

topicSummary: 這堂課是一段朋友之間的對話，討論各自的新年新希望——有人要戒掉讓自己痛苦的交友 App、有人要對老闆設立界線、有人要修正姿勢、有人要停止整天躺沙發⋯⋯幽默又超級生活化！課堂也會探討一個深層問題：為什麼我們每年立 flag 又每年失敗？改變習慣其實跟自我認同感有關。
discussion: 你最近有沒有想改變的壞習慣？你覺得難在哪裡？
你覺得新年新希望通常失敗的原因是什麼？
影片中的角色提到要「對老闆設立界線」——你覺得在生活或工作中設立界線，最難的部分是什麼？
你同意「改變習慣跟自我認同感有關」這個說法嗎？有沒有類似的親身經歷？
